reasoning: The CDC currently has a Level 2 Travel Health Notice (THN) for Colombia due to a yellow fever outbreak, which was just updated on April 15, 2025. Evaluating the criteria for Level 3 (Reconsider Nonessential Travel), the CDC heavily reserves this level for severe, unmitigable threats to US travelers, such as Ebola or uncontrolled global pandemics (like COVID-19). Because yellow fever is entirely vaccine-preventable, US travelers can protect themselves completely prior to departure by getting vaccinated. Historically, even massive yellow fever or dengue outbreaks (like Brazil in 2018) remained at Level 2, as the core advice to travelers stands to practice enhanced precautions—namely, getting the shot and preventing mosquito bites. Given the CDC just reaffirmed the Level 2 status days ago, and there are currently very few if any Level 3/4 THNs active globally, the probability of the CDC escalating this to Level 3 or higher by June 1, 2025 is very low.
